verdicts.note: The URL https://pathlit.mintlify.app/docs/nodes/pitchbook-agent does not point to an MCP server. It appears to be a PathLit integration or 'agent' node, not a dedicated Model Context Protocol server. Evidence only mentions dlthub's dlt MCP server in passing (for loading data pipelines), not a dedicated PitchBook MCP. Should be 'no' or 'unknown'.
The provided URL is not evidence of a dedicated PitchBook MCP server; it should be removed.
Evidence from dlthub and dev.writer.com confirms API key authentication using 'PB-Token {API_KEY}' in Authorization header, issued by account manager, not self-generated.
Evidence confirms no free tier, requires contacting PitchBook account manager/support for API access, and custom enterprise pricing. 'partnership-contact-sales' is accurate.
dlthub doc confirms REST API v2 with base URL https://api.pitchbook.com and standard REST endpoints.
Evidence lists endpoints: companies, companies/search, deals, deals/search, people, people/search, investors, funds, account/credits. 'medium' breadth is reasonable.
'buildable-with-caveats' is appropriate given the API exists but requires paid enterprise contract and manual account manager provisioning.
Evidence consistently states API key must be obtained via PitchBook account manager, no self-serve key generation. Blocker statement is accurate.
Evidence supports REST API with companies, deals, people, investors, funds endpoints; API key auth issued by account manager; enterprise-only access. One-liner accurately summarizes.
0.72 is reasonable given evidence from third-party integration docs (dlthub, Writer) but no direct PitchBook official API docs provided.
Flags correctly reflect no self-serve signup, credit-based pricing, and note that third-party integrations exist but no official first-party MCP is confirmed by evidence.

revised.name: PitchBook
revised.one_liner: PitchBook offers a REST API (companies, deals, people, investors, funds) authenticated via an API key issued by an account manager, but access is enterprise-only with no self-serve signup.

auth_methods.notes: Header format: Authorization: PB-Token {API_KEY}. Key issued by account manager, not self-generated via a dashboard signup flow.

revised.self_serve_notes: No free tier or self-serve API signup; must contact a PitchBook account manager, purchase a custom enterprise contract, and receive credits/API key. Each request consumes purchased credits.

api_surface.notes: Base URL https://api.pitchbook.com with endpoints for companies, companies/search, deals, deals/search, people, people/search, investors, funds, and account/credits.

revised.main_blocker: API access requires a paid enterprise contract and manual provisioning by a PitchBook account manager; no self-serve key generation, which limits automated onboarding for an agent toolkit.

evidence.supports: Describes REST API v2, base URL, endpoints, PB-Token auth header, credit-based usage, and manual key provisioning via account manager.
Confirms API key authentication and that access requires contacting PitchBook support/account for API key generation.
Indicates no free tier and enterprise-level custom pricing for PitchBook.
